Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex [The attackers, part of a basic skill level group known for the 2022 Ukraine Electric Power Attack, launch a multi-stage complex cyber assault targeting transportation systems. They employ a wide range of tactics from reconnaissance to impact, aiming to disrupt operations and exfiltrate sensitive data. Their methods involve exploiting network vulnerabilities, maintaining persistence, escalating privileges, evading defenses, accessing credentials, discovering network configurations, moving laterally, collecting sensitive data, and finally impacting the system to inhibit recovery.]

threat_actor_profile.campaign_or_group: name=2022 Ukraine Electric Power Attack Group

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Network Traffic, Command & Control Server, Application Log, Public IP/Network Block, User Account, File System, Network Traffic, DNS Record, Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), Response Metadata (Mechanism), Bash Script, Web Shell, Application Log Content (Mechanism), User Account Authentication (Mechanism), System Network Configuration Discovery, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), Logon Session Creation (Mechanism), Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol, Process Creation (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309 Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: The attackers initiate active scanning to map out the network topology of the transportation system, identifying potential entry points and critical infrastructure.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522 Name: Malicious Hardware Component Replacement
stage.description: The attackers acquire or compromise servers to establish command and control infrastructure, which will be used throughout the attack lifecycle.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 535 Name: Malicious Gray Market Hardware
stage.description: Employees visiting malicious websites unknowingly download malware, leading to initial access onto the transportation system's network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 88 Name: OS Command Injection
stage.description: Using command-line interfaces, the attackers execute payloads to gain a foothold within the system.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 650 Name: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: To maintain persistent access, the attackers deploy web shells on compromised servers, allowing them to execute arbitrary commands remotely.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers search for and exploit vulnerabilities to escalate their privileges, gaining administrative control over key systems.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 578 Name: Disable Security Software
stage.description: To evade detection, the attackers impair security tools by disabling or modifying them, ensuring their actions remain undetected.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 49 Name: Password Brute Forcing
stage.description: The attackers attempt to gain access to valid credentials through brute force attacks on user accounts.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309 Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: The attackers identify network topology and services to understand the layout and plan further lateral movement.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 324 Name: TCP (ISN) Sequence Predictability Probe
stage.description: Utilizing remote service protocols, the attackers move laterally across the network to reach critical systems.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 277 Name: Data Interchange Protocol Manipulation
stage.description: The attackers extract sensitive data from internal repositories, focusing on valuable operational and strategic information.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 590 Name: IP Address Blocking
stage.description: Data is transferred over alternative protocols, such as DNS, to obscure the exfiltration process and avoid detection.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers destroy backups and manipulate system settings to inhibit recovery efforts, effectively crippling the transportation system's operations.

Scenario.summary: The attackers meticulously planned and executed a sophisticated cyber operation against a transportation system, employing a broad spectrum of tactics from reconnaissance to impact. Their approach involved mapping the network, establishing persistent access, escalating privileges, evading defenses, and ultimately disrupting the system while exfiltrating sensitive data. The attackers leveraged multiple vectors and surfaces, demonstrating a high level of planning and adaptability.
